title: Project Report: Real-Time Streaming Pipeline — Data Engineer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Kafka → Spark Structured Streaming → Delta Lake with monitoring and exactly-once semantics.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Advanced)*

**Real-Time Streaming Pipeline** is a advanced-level end-to-end project for Data Engineer. Duration: 6–8 weeks. Kafka → Spark Structured Streaming → Delta Lake with monitoring and exactly-once semantics. Tools: Kafka, Spark, Delta Lake, Grafana.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Kafka topic ingestion
Objective: Spark streaming transforms
Objective: Delta Lake sink
Objective: Grafana monitoring dashboard
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Data Engineer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Data Engineer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Data Engineer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Kafka
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Kafka** *(Level: Advanced)*

Producer + consumer. Complete these tasks: Avro schema; Partitions. Deliverable for this phase: Topic flowing.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Avro schema
- [ ] Partitions
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Kafka is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Data Engineer agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Topic flowing to the teacher.

chapters.title: Phase: Spark
chapters.content:

```text
- [ ] Watermark
```

chapters.title: Phase: Delta
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Delta** *(Level: Advanced)*

ACID sink. Complete these tasks: Merge upserts; Time travel query. Deliverable for this phase: Delta table.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Merge upserts
- [ ] Time travel query
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Delta is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 3 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Data Engineer agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example

chapters.title: Phase: Monitor
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Monitor** *(Level: Advanced)*

Metrics dashboard. Complete these tasks: Lag; Throughput; Errors. Deliverable for this phase: Grafana dashboard.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Lag
- [ ] Throughput
- [ ] Errors
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### When to use it

### Where to use it

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Advanced)*

Submit all final deliverables: Streaming job code; Delta table; Grafana dashboard; Architecture doc. Skills demonstrated: Kafka, Spark, Streaming. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Streaming job code
Deliverable: Delta table
Deliverable: Grafana dashboard
Deliverable: Architecture doc
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Streaming job code and passes the rubric.
